best_extraction.title: Four-Extremity Neurostimulation Using Two Cervical Octapolar Leads and High Frequency of 10 kHz
best_extraction.study_question: Can 10-kHz cervical SCS relieve four-extremity neuropathic pain without paresthesias?
best_extraction.population_assessed: 29-year-old man with C3 fracture, central cord lesion, four-extremity neuropathic pain after cervical injury.
best_extraction.outcome_measures: NRS pain score, functional improvement, opioid use, paresthesia presence
best_extraction.inclusion_criteria: Single patient with refractory four-extremity neuropathic pain after cervical injury and surgery.
best_extraction.answer_to_study_question: 10-kHz cervical SCS reduced pain in all extremities, improved function, and avoided paresthesias through 3 months.

reasoning: All models strongly agreed on bibliographic fields and study design. I merged consistent clinical details, favoring shared high-confidence wording for outcomes and population.

field_level_decisions.population_assessed: Merged consensus from all three; retained C3 fracture and central cord lesion details.
field_level_decisions.inclusion_criteria: Used OpenRouter phrasing because case reports describe patient selection broadly, not literal trial criteria.
field_level_decisions.answer_to_study_question: Used consensus summary without exact percentages to stay concise and robust.
